title: Conjecture 1 (Cereceda)
statement_text: If $G$ is a $k$-degenerate graph on $n$ vertices, then $R_{k+2}(G)$ has diameter $O(n^2)$.
context_text: Cereceda conjectured this quadratic diameter bound for the reconfiguration graph with $k+2$ colors on $k$-degenerate graphs, and the bound would be best possible. Despite several efforts and partial results, the conjecture remains open even for $k=2$.
paper_contribution: The paper does not directly prove any case of this conjecture; it is cited as foundational motivation for the study of reconfiguration of colorings of planar graphs, and the techniques developed (reducible configurations, valid recoloring sequences) are in the same research program.
paper_title: An update on reconfiguring $10$-colorings of planar graphs

title: Problem 3
statement_text: What is the minimum integer $\kappa$ such that for every planar graph $G$ with $n$ vertices, $R_{\kappa}(G)$ has diameter $O(n)$?
context_text: Planar graphs are 5-degenerate and have maximum average degree less than 6; prior results give $R_8(G)$ diameter $O(n(\log n)^7)$ and $R_{12}(G)$ diameter at most $6n$ for planar $G$. The paper improves the upper bound to $\kappa \leq 10$ via Theorem 4, and an isolated 6-coloring of the icosahedron shows $\kappa \geq 7$; the authors note that $\kappa = 7$ cannot be excluded.
paper_title: An update on reconfiguring $10$-colorings of planar graphs
